docs.title: Drummer
docs.text:
A local, standalone REST client — free and open-source alternative to Postman, Insomnia, and Bruno.

  • No account — runs entirely on your machine
  • No subscription — free forever
  • No phone-home — your data stays local
  • Git-friendly — request files are plain Markdown with YAML frontmatter, fully diffable

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
Homebrew (macOS):

brew tap ceautery/drummer
brew install drummer

pip:

pip install drummer

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
drummer

Drummer opens in your browser at http://localhost:8000.

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
• Getting Started
  • CLI Reference
  • Scripting API
  • MCP Tools
  • Source on GitHub

docs.title: Attribution
docs.text: Drummer's built-in tutorials and mock servers include data from two open-access sources, each released under Creative Commons Zero (CC0). Both are used here for educational and demonstration purposes only.

docs.title: Metropolitan Museum of Art Open Access
docs.text:
Drummer's REST tutorial and mock server include data from the Metropolitan Museum of Art Open Access collection.

License: Creative Commons Zero (CC0) — no rights reserved Source: metmuseum.org/about-the-met/policies-and-documents/open-access

The Met makes its Open Access data available for unrestricted commercial and noncommercial use without requiring permission from the Museum.

docs.title: Wikidata
docs.text:
Drummer's GraphQL tutorial and mock GraphQL server include a snapshot of data from Wikidata.

License: Creative Commons Zero (CC0) — no rights reserved Source: wikidata.org

Wikidata makes its data available for unrestricted reuse.

docs.title: drummer
docs.text:
Start the Drummer server and open it in the browser.

drummer [OPTIONS]
Option Default Description --project PATH none Register an external folder as a workspace and open it --port INTEGER 8000 Port to listen on --host TEXT 127.0.0.1 Host address to bind to

Examples:

# Open Drummer (last workspace, or built-in Scratch on first run)
drummer

# Register and open an external project folder
drummer --project ~/projects/my-api

# Custom port
drummer --port 9000 --project ~/projects/my-api

docs.title: drummer serve
docs.text: Hidden alias for drummer (start the server). Prefer drummer directly.

docs.title: drummer new
docs.text:
Create a new workspace under ~/.drummer/projects/.

drummer new NAME

Creates a new central workspace directory under ~/.drummer/projects/<slug> with project.yaml and a default local environment.

docs.title: drummer export
docs.text:
Export a Drummer project as a zip file for sharing.

drummer export PATH

docs.title: drummer mcp
docs.text:
Print MCP server connection information.

drummer mcp

Outputs the URL and port to configure in Claude's MCP settings, and lists all available tools.

docs.title: Global flags
docs.text: Flag Description --version / -V Print version and exit --attribution Print dataset attribution (Metropolitan Museum of Art) and exit --help Show help for any command

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• macOS or Linux
  • Python 3.12+

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
Homebrew (macOS):

brew tap ceautery/drummer
brew install drummer

pip:

pip install drummer

docs.title: Create a project
docs.text:
A Drummer project is a folder with a .drummer/ directory. Create one by hand:

my-api/
├── .drummer/
│   ├── project.yaml
│   └── environments/
│       └── local.yaml
└── list-users.md

.drummer/project.yaml

name: My API
version: "1"
default_environment: local

.drummer/environments/local.yaml

name: local
variables:
  base_url: https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com

list-users.md

---
name: List Users
method: GET
url: "{{base_url}}/users"
---

docs.title: Start Drummer
docs.text:
drummer --project /path/to/my-api

Drummer starts on port 8000. Open http://localhost:8000 in your browser. Select your environment in the sidebar, click Send, and see the response.

docs.title: Built-in tutorial
docs.text: Run drummer, then click the Tutorial button in the top bar. The tutorial walks through all of Drummer's features using an offline mock API — no internet required.

docs.title: What's next
docs.text:
• CLI Reference — all commands and flags
  • Scripting API — pre/post-request JavaScript
  • MCP Tools — connect Claude to Drummer

docs.title: MCP Tools
docs.text: Drummer exposes a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server so Claude can send requests, inspect responses, and manage environments directly.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
1. Start Drummer: drummer
  2. Add to your Claude MCP configuration:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "drummer": {
      "url": "http://localhost:8000/mcp"
    }
  }
}

Run drummer mcp to confirm the URL and list available tools.

docs.title: Available tools
docs.text: Tool Description list_projects List all known projects with metadata list_requests Get the request tree for a project get_request Get the parsed definition of a request create_request Create a new request file update_request Update fields in an existing request send_request Fire a request; returns status, headers, body, timing, script output get_history Recent response history for a request list_environments List environments for a project get_variables Get variables for a named environment set_variable Set a variable in the active environment switch_environment Change the active environment clear_cookies Clear the session cookie store

docs.title: Example workflow
docs.text:
list_projects
→ [{ "name": "My API", "id": "my-api" }]

list_requests { "project_id": "my-api" }
→ [{ "path": "users/list-users.md", "name": "List Users" }]

send_request { "project_id": "my-api", "path": "users/list-users.md" }
→ { "status_code": 200, "elapsed_ms": 142, "body": "[{\"id\":1,...}]" }

set_variable { "project_id": "my-api", "env": "local", "key": "base_url", "value": "https://staging.example.com" }

send_request { "project_id": "my-api", "path": "users/list-users.md" }
→ { "status_code": 200, "elapsed_ms": 98, "body": "[...]" }

docs.title: Scripting API
docs.text: Drummer runs JavaScript pre-request and post-request scripts using QuickJS — a lightweight embedded JS engine. Scripts access the dm global object.

docs.title: dm.env
docs.text:
Read and write active environment variables.

// Read
const token = dm.env.get("token");    // returns string or null

// Write (persists for the session)
dm.env.set("user_id", body.id.toString());

docs.title: dm.request
docs.text:
Available in pre-request scripts only. Changes take effect on the outgoing request.

Property Type Description dm.request.url string Request URL dm.request.method string HTTP method dm.request.headers object Request headers (mutable) dm.request.params object Query parameters (mutable) dm.request.body string Request body (mutable)

Example — add a timestamp header:

dm.request.headers["X-Timestamp"] = Date.now().toString();

docs.title: dm.response
docs.text:
Available in post-request scripts only. Read-only.

Property/Method Type Description dm.response.status number HTTP status code dm.response.json() any Parsed JSON body (throws if not JSON) dm.response.text() string Raw response body dm.response.headers object Response headers

Example — extract an ID from the response:

const body = dm.response.json();
dm.env.set("created_id", body.id.toString());

docs.title: dm.console
docs.text:
dm.console.log("debug info", someValue);

All console.log output is captured and shown in the Script Output panel, even if the script fails or times out.

docs.title: Limits
docs.text: Constraint Value Timeout 5 seconds Network access Not available Filesystem access Not available

docs.title: Script debugger
docs.text:
When a script fails, Drummer shows an actionable suggestion alongside the error:

Situation Suggestion shown TypeError: undefined is not an object Response may not be JSON — check Content-Type, try dm.response.text() first dm.env.get("X") returns null Variable X is not set in the active environment Script timeout Check for infinite loops Parse error Exact line/column with code excerpt Uncaught exception Full QuickJS stack trace, syntax-highlighted
